# Verify `sesam user add-recipient` adds a second recipient (device key) to a
# user and re-wraps the audit key so the new key can decrypt the log.

exec git init
exec git config user.email test@sesam.dev
exec git config user.name 'Sesam Test'

exec sesam init --user admin
exec git add -A
exec git commit -m 'init sesam'

# Add bob as a dev with a single recipient.
exec sesam tell --user bob --recipient $BOB_PUBKEY --group dev

# Carol's key cannot decrypt the log yet - she is not a recipient of anyone.
! exec sesam --identity $CAROL_KEY show .sesam/audit/log.jsonl

# Add carol's key as a second recipient for bob (e.g. a new device).
exec sesam user add-recipient --user bob --recipient $CAROL_PUBKEY

# The audit log records the operation...
exec sesam show .sesam/audit/log.jsonl
stdout '"user.add_recipients"'
stdout '"bob"'

# ...and `sesam log` renders it with the add glyph and description.
exec sesam log
stdout 'add recipient of bob'

# The newly added key can now decrypt the audit log (WriteAuditKey re-wrapped it).
exec sesam --identity $CAROL_KEY show .sesam/audit/log.jsonl
stdout '"user.add_recipients"'

exec sesam verify

# Adding a recipient to a non-existent user fails.
! exec sesam user add-recipient --user ghost --recipient $CAROL_PUBKEY
